page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-48-lssd is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It is equipped with 48 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture and provides 192.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU, aligning with standard general-purpose resource allocations.

This instance configuration does not include any graphics processing units and lists local storage as none, meaning it relies on external block storage for its operating system and data volumes. Additionally, the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external networking configurations or NAT gateways for public internet connectivity.

Due to the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource distribution, this server is designed for predictable multi-threaded workloads. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, enterprise application hosting, and medium-scale database workloads that require a steady balance of compute and memory without the need for local SSD storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-48-lssd is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the c4a server family, featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory. This configuration delivers a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it highly suitable for general-purpose workloads. The instance does not feature integrated GPUs or local storage, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, necessitating external network and storage configurations. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the c4a-standard-48-lssd provides stable and predictable performance for multi-threaded applications. It is well-suited for web serving, enterprise application hosting, and general-purpose computing tasks that require a reliable balance of compute and memory resources without specialized hardware acceleration.
